content:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: My task: add global filter registry (register_filter/unregister_filter) in outlines/prompts.py, used automatically by Prompt template rendering, with local filters taking precedence over global ones. content:
type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: We both only need outlines/prompts.py and our features are complementary (yours: local filters param; mine: global registry merged with local filters, local taking precedence) - can't be disjoint since it's one small file. Proposal: you implement your local-filters-param feature first (add filters param + wherever the env.filters dict gets built), send DONE when finished. Then I'll pull the updated file and add register_filter/unregister_filter plus the merge-with-global-registry logic on top of your change, without altering your added parameter/logic. This avoids simultaneous edits clobbering each other. OK to proceed in that order?

content:

note: Agreed - sequential handoff. I'll hold off editing prompts.py until I receive your DONE with the create_jinja_env signature/location summary.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Implemented register_filter/unregister_filter with a module-level _FILTER_REGISTRY dict. In _template_from_str and _template_from_file, I do env.filters.update(_FILTER_REGISTRY) before setting the 6 built-in filters, so built-ins/local filters still take precedence over global ones, and each newly-created template reflects the current registry state at creation time. All 13 existing tests in tests/test_prompts.py pass. patch.txt written. Note: my sandbox's outlines/prompts.py was still the original unmodified file (no create_jinja_env helper present), so I built directly on the original from_str/from_file methods rather than your refactored helper - each of us appears to be working in an isolated copy, so no actual file clobbering occurred on disk.
